title: Meum alatum

herb_names_common.name: meum (conflict with none, but not widely used)

uses: Meum alatum, also known as winged meum, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. There is limited information available on its specific medicinal uses, but it has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential health benefits. Some sources suggest that it may be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Meum alatum.
taxonomy: Meum alatum is classified as a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Meum and is a member of the order Apiales. The species is native to Europe and Asia, where it can be found growing in woodland edges and other moist areas. Meum alatum is often referred to as winged meum due to the winged or wing-like shape of its stems. It is a perennial plant that is relatively small in size, typically growing to be around 30-60 centimeters tall.
benefits: Meum alatum, also known as winged birthwort, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Its leaves and stems are used in herbal remedies, often in combination with other plants. Meum alatum is believed to have a diuretic effect, which may aid in treating urinary issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Meum alatum for medicinal purposes.
properties: Meum alatum is a plant traditionally used in folk medicine. Meum alatum contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Its therapeutic properties are also thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
